title: campanula celsii subsp. spathulifolia
intro: Campanula celsii subsp. spathulifolia, also known as the Celsian bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The Celsian bellflower has been traditionally used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of Campanula celsii subsp. spathulifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
